subsections.title: What is PermaClaw?
subsections.content:
PermaClaw is an ultra‑lightweight, self‑hostable AI assistant with permanent, wallet‑recoverable memory.

It combines a minimal Go agent, a rich web UI, a decentralized app store (aoStore), and permanent storage on the AO network.

  • Never lose your memories – all conversations, neuron spike data, and learned facts are stored in your own AO process.
  • Recover with just your wallet – lose your device? Point to your wallet file and everything is back.
  • Built‑in app store – browse and install Devices (skills), DApps, and Projects directly from the AO ecosystem.

subsections.title: Installation
subsections.content:
Prerequisites

  • Linux, macOS, or Windows (with WSL)
  • Go 1.22+ (only needed if building from source)
  • An Arweave wallet (e.g. aos.json – see arweave.org)

Download the Pre‑built Binary

# Linux (amd64)
curl -L https://github.com/yourname/permaclaw/releases/latest/download/permaclaw-linux-amd64 -o permaclaw
chmod +x permaclaw
./permaclaw

Build from Source

git clone https://github.com/Ar-Aostore/PermaClaw.git 
cd permaclaw
make build
./bin/permaclaw

subsections.title: Quick Start
subsections.content:
After running PermaClaw for the first time, open the web UI at http://localhost:18800.

  1. Go to Settings and provide the path to your Arweave wallet (e.g. /home/user/.aos.json).
  2. Click Spawn Memory Process – this creates your permanent AO process and saves its ID in the config.
  3. Start chatting! Your memories are now automatically saved to your AO process.

You can also do this via the command line:

permaclaw spawn-memory --wallet /path/to/wallet.json

subsections.title: Getting Started
subsections.content:
Once your wallet is connected and memory process spawned, you can start using PermaClaw immediately.

Basic Commands

  • /help – show available commands
  • /model <name> – switch AI model
  • /clear – clear conversation history

You can also ask the agent to perform tasks like web search, file operations, etc.

subsections.title: Using aoStore & Skills
subsections.content:
The Store tab in the web UI lists products from the aoStore process.

  • Devices are installable skills. Click Install – the skill package is downloaded from Arweave and placed in your workspace under skills/. The agent can then use it immediately.
  • DApps can be launched in an embedded iframe or external browser.
  • Projects show token and protocol information.

subsections.title: Chat Channels
subsections.content:
PermaClaw supports multiple chat platforms. Configure them in ~/.permaclaw/config.json.

{
  "channels": {
    "telegram": {
      "enabled": true,
      "token": "YOUR_BOT_TOKEN"
    },
    "discord": {
      "enabled": true,
      "token": "YOUR_BOT_TOKEN"
    }
  }
}

After configuration, run permaclaw and the channels will be active.

subsections.title: CL1 Setup
subsections.content:
PermaClaw includes a built‑in client for streaming CL1 neuron activity to your memory process.

Prerequisites

  • CL1 device or CL SDK Simulator installed
  • Python 3.8+ with cl-sdk package

Install the Python SDK:

pip install cl-sdk

subsections.title: Streaming Neuron Data
subsections.content:
Example Python script that sends spike data to PermaClaw’s API:

import requests
import time

while True:
    spike = {
        "timestamp": time.time_ns(),
        "channel": 42,
        "amplitude": 1.2
    }
    requests.post("http://localhost:18800/api/memory/spike", json=spike)
    time.sleep(0.1)

The spike is automatically forwarded to your AO process and becomes part of your permanent memory.

subsections.title: Data Analysis
subsections.content:
Later, you can query the memory process via the UI or API to retrieve all recorded spikes for analysis.

GET /api/memory?from=2025-01-01&to=2025-01-02

This returns a JSON array of spike events, which you can then process with your own tools.
